emails.subject: Your assessment results — a few patterns stood out, [FIRST_NAME]
emails.preview: What your score is actually telling us
emails.body:
Hi [FIRST_NAME] — quick note from Dr. Mele at Alive Integrative Medicine.

I reviewed your Custom Health Assessment, and a few patterns came up immediately:

* Primary focus: [CORE_ISSUE_1]
* Secondary contributors: [CORE_ISSUE_2], [CORE_ISSUE_3]
* Most common symptoms people with your pattern report: [SYMPTOM_A], [SYMPTOM_B], [SYMPTOM_C]

Here is what often gets missed: healing is not about chasing the loudest symptom. It is about understanding the full picture — your history, your biology, your context — and building a plan that fits you specifically.

The 3 things that tend to create real momentum for people with [CORE_ISSUE_1]:

1. Clarify what is actually driving it (not just what it looks like on the surface)
2. Restore the depleted system thoughtfully and at a pace your body can use
3. Adjust as you respond — because healing is not linear

Our practice in Eugene is built for people who have tried the standard approach and need something more thorough.

Your next step:
* Option A: See your recommended first steps inside the app → [VIEW_MY_PLAN_LINK]
* Option B: Book a new patient visit and we will work through your pattern together → [SCHEDULE_LINK]

Your assessment is already a head start.

— Dr. Brielle Mele, ND
Alive Integrative Medicine | Eugene, OR | aliveintegrative.com

emails.subject: Why [PRIMARY_SYMPTOM] often outlasts every protocol you have tried
emails.preview: The part of the picture most people never get
emails.body:
Hi [FIRST_NAME],

There is a pattern I see often, and I want to explain it plainly.

Standard medicine is designed to diagnose and treat conditions that show up on conventional testing. It does that well. What it is not designed for is the space between 'diagnosed' and 'thriving' — the persistent fatigue, the mood swings, the symptoms that are real but do not land cleanly on a test result.

Naturopathic and functional medicine approaches that space differently. Instead of asking 'what is your diagnosis,' we ask 'what systems are under-supported, and what does your body need to do the work of healing?'

For someone with a pattern like yours — where [PRIMARY_SYMPTOM] is a central piece — the underlying contributors often involve things like dysregulated cortisol patterns, micronutrient depletions, or immune and gut signals that have not been assessed.

When those go unaddressed, the symptom persists — not because you are not trying, but because the root cause was never part of the conversation.

At Alive Integrative, that is exactly where we start.

— Dr. Brielle Mele, ND
Alive Integrative Medicine | Eugene, OR | aliveintegrative.com

[Book a New Patient Visit →]([SCHEDULE_LINK])

emails.subject: A patient with a similar pattern — what shifted for them
emails.body:
Hi [FIRST_NAME],

I want to share a brief story — one that echoes what I see in assessments like yours.

A patient came to our practice after several years of managing symptoms that never fully resolved. Fatigue, irregular cycles, and persistent brain fog. Conventional workups were unremarkable. She had tried elimination diets, multiple supplements, and a round of hormone therapy with limited results.

During her intake, we took a full history — not just labs, but sleep patterns, stress load, gut history, and a detailed timeline. A more comprehensive functional panel identified two specific gaps that had not been addressed.

We built a phased plan. Slow, targeted, adjusted as she responded.

Several months later, she described herself as feeling 'like herself again for the first time in years.'

Individual results vary and depend on many factors unique to each person. But the approach is consistent: understand the full picture, address the actual root, and give the body what it needs to respond.

If your assessment reflects a similar pattern, I would be glad to work through it with you.

— Dr. Brielle Mele, ND
Alive Integrative Medicine | Eugene, OR | aliveintegrative.com

Disclaimer: Individual results vary. This is not medical advice.

[Schedule Your Visit →]([SCHEDULE_LINK])

gamma_content.digital_health_report:
# 🔎 DIGITAL SEO HEALTH REPORT — aliveintegrative.com
━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━

## 📊 DOMAIN SNAPSHOT
──────────────────

| Metric | Value |
|---|---|
| Global Rank | #922,344 |
| Keywords Ranked | 667 |
| Monthly Traffic | ~1,235 visits |
| Traffic Value | $465/mo |
| Keywords in Top 10 | 18 |
| SERP Features Traffic | ~0 visits/mo |

---

## 🔑 THE CORE FINDING: RANKED BUT INVISIBLE — PAGE 2 IS A TRAFFIC DEAD ZONE
──────────────────────────────────────────────────────

Alive Integrative Medicine ranks for 667 keywords yet converts almost none of them into traffic. Here's why:

- **Pos #1:** 3 keywords
- **Pos #2–3:** 2 keywords
- **Pos #4–10:** 13 keywords
- **Pos #11–20:** 32 keywords
- **SERP Features Traffic:** ~0 visits/mo

32 keywords are trapped on page 2 — statistically invisible to patients — while SERP features that could amplify all 18 top-10 rankings deliver zero additional traffic.

---

## 🤖 AI SEARCH GAP
──────────────────

Google triggered AI Overviews on **8 SERPs** where this practice already ranks. Alive Integrative Medicine was cited in **0** of them. That's 8 opportunities to appear in the answer patients read first — before they ever click a result — all currently going to competitors.

---

## 🎯 BIGGEST OPPORTUNITY: "NATUROPATHIC NEAR ME" LOCAL INTENT CLUSTER
──────────────────────────────────────────────────────

- [pos 18] naturopathic near me — 4,400/mo | KD: 51
- [pos 19] naturopathic dr near me — 1,900/mo | KD: 37
- [pos 11] natropathic doctor near me — 1,600/mo | KD: 50
- [pos 14] natural doctor near me — 880/mo | KD: 51
- [pos 17] naturalpathic doctor near me — 720/mo | KD: 51
- [pos 14] naturopathic practitioner near me — 390/mo | KD: 38

Over **9,890 combined monthly searches** from patients actively looking for exactly what this practice offers — and not one of these keywords ranks above position 11.

---

## ⚡ WITHIN STRIKING DISTANCE (pos 4–10)
──────────────────────────────────────

| Keyword | Position | Volume | KD |
|---|---|---|---|
| alive medicine | 8 | 880/mo | 34 |
| myers push iv | 5 | 480/mo | 19 |
| myers push | 4 | 390/mo | 15 |
| massage therapist eugene oregon | 10 | 320/mo | 32 |
| massage time therapy | 10 | 320/mo | 12 |
| alive massage | 6 | 260/mo | 24 |

→ **Highest priority:** 'myers push' (pos 4, KD: 15) and 'myers push iv' (pos 5, KD: 19) are low-competition terms one optimization away from a top-3 ranking — a service no major competitor appears to be contesting.

---

## 🏆 VS YOUR TOP COMPETITOR
──────────────────────────

| Metric | aliveintegrative.com | oregonnaturopathicclinic.com |
|---|---|---|
| Keywords Ranked | 667 | 297 |
| Monthly Traffic | ~1,235 | ~149 |
| Traffic Value | $465/mo | $100/mo |

Alive Integrative Medicine already outranks its closest naturopathic competitor on every measurable — the gap just needs to be pressed.

---

## 🎯 3-PRIORITY ATTACK PLAN
──────────────────────────

1. **CAPTURE THE 'NEAR ME' CLUSTER** — Optimize dedicated service pages and Google Business Profile for the 9,890 monthly searches across six naturopathic 'near me' variants currently stuck between positions 11–20; target top-5 within 90 days.

2. **LOCK DOWN THE MYERS PUSH RANKINGS** — Strengthen the existing page targeting 'myers push' (pos 4, KD: 15) and 'myers push iv' (pos 5, KD: 19) to secure top-3 positions on two keywords competitors are not contesting; target within 30 days.

3. **CLOSE THE AI OVERVIEW GAP** — The practice appears in 8 AI Overview SERPs and is cited in 0; add an llms.txt file (currently NOT FOUND), ensure AI crawlers are permitted, and structure key service pages to answer the exact questions those 8 searches are asking; target at least 2 citations within 60 days.

I want to share a brief story — one that echoes what I see in assessments like yours.

A patient came to our practice after several years of managing symptoms that never fully resolved. Fatigue, irregular cycles, and persistent brain fog. Conventional workups were unremarkable. She had tried elimination diets, multiple supplements, and a round of hormone therapy with limited results.

During her intake, we took a full history — not just labs, but sleep patterns, stress load, gut history, and a detailed timeline. A more comprehensive functional panel identified two specific gaps that had not been addressed.

We built a phased plan. Slow, targeted, adjusted as she responded.

Several months later, she described herself as feeling 'like herself again for the first time in years.'

Individual results vary and depend on many factors unique to each person. But the approach is consistent: understand the full picture, address the actual root, and give the body what it needs to respond.

If your assessment reflects a similar pattern, I would be glad to work through it with you.

— Dr. Brielle Mele, ND
Alive Integrative Medicine | Eugene, OR | aliveintegrative.com

careModels.name: Nutrition Tune-Up (2 Month)
careModels.desc: Two visits including a 60-minute initial and 30-minute follow-up, plus 8 weeks of personalized meal planning.

careModels.name: Nutrition Recharge (4 Month)
careModels.desc: Four visits with in-depth support for weight loss, GI concerns, cholesterol, diabetes, and nutrient deficiencies. Includes 4 months of meal planning.

careModels.name: Meal Planning — 1 Week
careModels.desc: Includes a 15-minute evaluation and a one-week personalized meal plan.

careModels.name: Meal Planning — 4 Weeks
careModels.desc: A four-week personalized meal plan with initial evaluation. Best for sustained dietary change.

careModels.name: Meal Plan Add-On — 1 Week
careModels.desc: Add an extra week of meal planning to any existing package.

careModels.name: Meal Plan Add-On — 4 Weeks
careModels.desc: Add four additional weeks of meal planning to an existing package.

careModels.name: Naturopathic Primary Care
careModels.desc: Individualized primary care including wellness exams, acute care, and chronic condition management with an integrative approach.

careModels.name: Acupuncture — Five Element
careModels.desc: Whole-person acupuncture addressing body, mind, and emotions at the root cause level using Five Element principles.

careModels.name: Massage Therapy
careModels.desc: Therapeutic massage for recovery, rehabilitation, or relaxation as a standalone or adjunctive treatment.

careModels.name: Functional Lab Testing
careModels.desc: Specialized testing for hormones, neurotransmitters, genetics, digestive function, and micronutrients to identify root causes.
